summary: Not a traditional full-time job — between January 2011 and September 2013, I worked with Express Employment Professionals as a temporary associate while actively working toward a career in Information Technology. They called on me regularly for short-term assignments because I was dependable, adaptable, and quick to learn. The work varied widely: data entry, general office administration, customer service (including work supporting the Department of Veterans Affairs), and frequent bilingual Spanish translation and interpretation, from interviewing Spanish-speaking individuals to translating documents between English and Spanish. Every assignment meant learning new processes and business operations with very little onboarding — an adaptability that later became one of my greatest strengths in IT consulting and systems administration. This period overlapped with my studies at the University of Phoenix, where I was pursuing Information Technology and computer programming; evenings and weekends went to learning networking, operating systems, and programming concepts. The flexibility of temp work gave me the room to keep studying while gaining professional experience across a range of business environments. Looking back, this wasn't just a collection of temporary jobs — it was a period of preparation that strengthened my communication, professionalism, and adaptability while my education built the technical foundation for the IT career that followed.
highlights: Worked as a temporary associate through Express from January 2011 to September 2013, taking on short-term assignments across Los Angeles and Orange County whenever they needed someone dependable and quick to ramp up.
Rotated across data entry, general office administration, and customer service assignments, including work supporting the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Called on frequently for Spanish translation and interpretation work — interviewing Spanish-speaking individuals and translating documents between English and Spanish.

Learned to become productive in a new environment with minimal onboarding, assignment after assignment — adaptability that became one of my greatest strengths in later IT consulting and systems administration work.

Used the flexibility of temp work to attend the University of Phoenix, studying Information Technology and computer programming — building the technical foundation for my IT career on evenings and weekends.
